title: The Feed-Forward Layer
concept: The Position-Wise Feed-Forward network processes each token's representation individually and identically, adding non-linear capacity.

exercises.prompt: What is the main job of the Position-Wise Feed-Forward network in a Transformer?

options.text: Process each token individually and identically.
Help tokens talk to their neighbors.

exercises.explain: In Transformers, attention layers help words connect, while feed-forward layers process each word on its own. This lesson focuses on the solo processor: the Feed-Forward layer.

cards.title: After the Talk, the Deep Think
cards.body: We know attention layers help words 'talk' to each other. But once they finish talking, how does each word digest that information and grow smarter? That is where the Feed-Forward layer steps in, giving each word its own private thinking space!

cards.title: What is the Feed-Forward Layer?
cards.body: The Feed-Forward Layer is a part of a Transformer that processes every single word (token) in a sentence individually and identically. It doesn't let tokens talk to each other; instead, it focuses on upgrading each token's individual features using non-linear math.

cards.title: The Solo Study Desk
cards.body: Think of a classroom of students writing an essay. The teacher walks around and hands each student a personal dictionary. Each student works on their own paper, using their own dictionary, without talking to their neighbors. They all get the exact same helpful tool, but they use it on their own work.

cards.title: Processing 'The Cat Sat'
cards.body: In the sentence 'The cat sat', the words 'The', 'cat', and 'sat' are processed by the Feed-Forward layer. The layer runs the word 'The' through a set of neural network formulas, then runs 'cat' through the *exact same* formulas, and finally 'sat', all without mixing their information.

cards.title: Myth: It Connects the Words
cards.body: Many people think that because it's called a 'network,' it must connect the words together. Actually, the Feed-Forward layer has zero communication between words! That job belongs entirely to the Attention layer. The Feed-Forward layer is strictly solo.

cards.title: Attention vs. Feed-Forward
cards.body: Let's compare how Attention layers and Feed-Forward layers handle tokens differently.

left.text: Tokens interact, share information, and connect with other words in the sentence.

right.text: Each token is processed in isolation. No sharing or talking allowed.

exercises.prompt: How does the Feed-Forward layer treat different tokens?

options.text: It applies the same formulas to each token separately.
It mixes the tokens together to create new words.
It deletes unimportant tokens from the sentence.
It changes the order of the tokens in the sentence.

exercises.explain: The Feed-Forward layer acts like an individual upgrade station. It uses the exact same neural network formulas on every single token, one by one.

exercises.prompt: True or False: The Feed-Forward layer is where tokens exchange information with neighboring tokens.
exercises.explain: The Feed-Forward layer does not allow tokens to share information; it processes them in isolation. Attention layers are used for sharing information.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe how the Feed-Forward layer processes tokens.
exercises.sentence: The Feed-Forward layer processes each token ________ and identically.
exercises.explain: The Feed-Forward layer does not let tokens share information. It processes each token completely independently using the same formulas.

exercises.prompt: Which system represents a Position-Wise Feed-Forward network?

options.text: System A: 'Reads all tokens at once and mixes their meanings together to generate a single summary word.'
System B: 'Takes Token 1 and processes it, then takes Token 2 and processes it using the same formula, keeping them separate.'
System C: 'Compares Token 1 to Token 2 and decides which one is more important to keep.'

exercises.explain: System A is incorrect because the Feed-Forward layer does not mix token information; that is the job of the attention mechanism. System B correctly shows independent processing.

exercises.prompt: Your AI model needs to process the words in a sentence using its Feed-Forward layer. What exactly does this layer do to the tokens?

options.text: It helps tokens share information with their left and right neighbors.
It processes each token's data completely independently using the same rules.
It changes its internal rules depending on where the token is in the sentence.
It acts as a memory bank to store past chat conversations.

exercises.explain: The Position-Wise Feed-Forward network treats every token like an isolated unit. It runs the exact same mathematical formulas on each token's data, completely ignoring what the other tokens are doing at that moment.
